visual_narrative: Dramatic thumbs-down warning gesture -> cycling through jaime-roig streets near find location -> item shot near trash revealing suspicious street furniture -> closer examination detail shot -> pivot to a real find with phone documentation -> fast cycling educational sequence on what to check -> calm ending with camera rising to sky
location_shown: Camí Fondo area (jaime-roig clips for office chair context, sant-antoni for pivot find, trinitat for ending)

clips.filename: [hook] cycling on the street, dowing my both thumbs down - quatre carreres.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Both thumbs down while cycling - perfect 'don't touch that!' warning energy for NO_TOQUEIS_ESO opening. Dramatic mood matches REPORTERO investigative tone

clips.filename: (calm) city street, tall buildings, scooter, parked cars, sidewalk, trees, camera movement - jaime-roig.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Cycling through jaime-roig streets - establishes the neighborhood where the office chair was found. Camera movement builds anticipation

clips.filename: [item] (calm) a person takes a photo of a green trash can, with a motorcycle parked nearby and a building in the background - jaime-roig.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Item documentation shot near trash area - shows the street furniture context. Person examining items near containers sets up the 'should you take it?' question

clips.filename: (calm) city street, white building, black lamppost, parked cars, bicycle, motion blur - jaime-roig.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Street context from the same jaime-roig area - visual continuity while narration explains what looks suspicious about the chair. Motion blur adds unease

clips.filename: [item] (calm) a building under construction, a parked car, a bicycle, a street lamp, a person's hand - jaime-roig.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Detail shot showing hand gesture near street items - construction context adds to 'things aren't always what they seem' narrative. Close-up perspective for examining damage points

clips.filename: (fast) city street with trees, buildings, and a bike - jaime-roig.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Fast cycling shot adds urgency to the warning section - 'what could go wrong' narration over dynamic footage. Energetic pacing matches REPORTERO investigation

clips.filename: [item] (calm) a person stands near a wooden cabinet, holding a phone with a camera, in a park-like setting - sant-antoni (1).mp4
clips.narrative_note: PIVOT moment - person documenting a GOOD find with phone camera. Shifts from warning to 'but THIS one is actually worth taking'. Phone documentation = educated finder

clips.filename: [item] (calm) wooden chest, legs, shoes, sidewalk, autumn leaves, sunlight - sant-antoni.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Good condition street furniture in nice lighting - visual proof that some finds ARE worth taking. Sunlight and autumn leaves make it feel inviting vs the earlier suspicious shots

clips.filename: [start] (calm) a city street with parked cars, a tall building, and a bicycle - jaime-roig.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Cycling through the area while narration lists the 3 things to check on an office chair: wheels, gas cylinder, upholstery. Calm pacing allows educational content to land

clips.filename: [item] (calm) a city street with parked cars, a blue recycling bin, and a bicycle rider's perspective - san-antonio.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Cycling past recycling containers - visual metaphor for knowing what's worth saving vs recycling. Supports educational criteria narration about office chair quality checks

clips.filename: [end] (calm) a city street lined with buildings, palm trees, and parked cars - exposicion.mp4
clips.narrative_note: Calm ending with palm trees and city buildings - gives breathing room for closing narration and CTA. Peaceful ending contrasts with the earlier warning energy
